general.title: telegram-cli — Command-line interface for Telegram

commands.init: Set up credentials and Telegram session
commands.whoami: Validate session and show user info
commands.send: Send a text message to a dialog
commands.backup: Backup message history from a dialog

general.footer: Run 'telegram-cli help <command>' for detailed help on a specific command.

init.title: init — Set up credentials and Telegram session
init.description: Interactively prompts for API ID, API hash, and phone number. Creates config.yaml and authenticates with Telegram (including 2FA if enabled).

whoami.title: whoami — Validate session and show user info
whoami.description: Connects to Telegram, validates the current session, and prints user ID, name, username, and phone.

ping.title: ping — Check Telegram availability
ping.description: Connects to Telegram and measures round-trip latency. Prints whether the service is reachable and the latency in milliseconds.

get-dialogs.title: get-dialogs — List dialogs
get-dialogs.description: Lists the user's dialogs (chats, groups, channels). Optionally limits the count and saves results to a JSON file in the output directory.

arguments.--limit: Maximum number of dialogs to return.

send.title: send — Send a text message
send.description: Sends a text message to the specified dialog. Prints the sent message as JSON (id, date, text).

arguments.dialog_id: Numeric dialog ID (use get-dialogs to find it).

edit.title: edit — Edit a message
edit.description: Edits a previously sent message. Prints the updated message as JSON.

arguments.message_id: ID of the message to edit.

delete.title: delete — Delete messages
delete.description: Deletes one or more of your own messages. Prints the list of deleted message IDs as JSON.

arguments.message_ids: One or more message IDs to delete.

backup.title: backup — Backup message history
backup.description: Retrieves messages from a dialog for full or incremental backup. Supports pagination with rate-limit handling, resumable backup, and structured progress output.

arguments.--since: ISO 8601 timestamp — only messages after this date (incremental).
arguments.--output: Write JSON output to this file instead of stdout.
arguments.--estimate: Print approximate time estimate and exit without downloading.

download-media.title: download-media — Download media from a message
download-media.description: Downloads the media file (photo, video, document) attached to a specific message. Prints file path, MIME type, and size.

arguments.message_id: ID of the message with media.

version.title: version — Show version
version.description: Prints CLI and library version information as JSON.

help.title: help — Show help
help.description: Displays help text. Optionally specify a language code (en, ru, fa, tr, ar, de) and/or a command name for detailed help.
